title: Legit Warning-LATCH is suppressed by incomplete case with default
body:
The following code produces a latch warning due to `c` being assigned only in `if (a)` branch. This is expected.

```SystemVerilog
module test (
    input        a,
    input [1:0]  b,
    output logic c
);
    always_comb
        if (a) begin
            case (b)
                2'd0, 2'd1, 2'd2, 2'd3: c = '0;
                default:;
            endcase
        end
endmodule
```

When running in debug mode ` verilator --lint-only -Wall --debug test.sv`:

```
- V3Active.cpp:626:   activeAll:
%Warning-LATCH: test.sv:6:5: Latch inferred for signal 'c' (not all control paths of combinational always assign a value)
                           : ... Suggest use of always_latch for intentional latches
-node: ALWAYS 0x5555555d3ad0 <e1997#> {e6af} [always_comb]
    6 |     always_comb
      |     ^~~~~~~~~~~
                ... For warning description see https://verilator.org/warn/LATCH?v=5.045
                ... Use "/* verilator lint_off LATCH */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
```

However, if the case is incomplete and `default` is present, then that warning is suppressed:

```SystemVerilog
case (b)
    2'd0, 2'd1, 2'd2: c = '0;
    default:;
endcase
```

```
- V3Active.cpp:626:   activeAll:
-arning-suppressed-LATCH: test.sv:6:5: Latch inferred for signal 'c' (not all control paths of combinational always assign a value)...
```

`Verilator 5.045 devel rev v5.044-54-g913cf933e`
OS: Arch Linux

EDIT:

Same thing happens even without `default`, if case item is unsized (32 bit) constant: 

```SystemVerilog
case (b)
    0: c = '0;
endcase
```
code_blocks:
module test (
    input        a,
    input [1:0]  b,
    output logic c
);
    always_comb
        if (a) begin
            case (b)
                2'd0, 2'd1, 2'd2, 2'd3: c = '0;
                default:;
            endcase
        end
endmodule
- V3Active.cpp:626:   activeAll:
%Warning-LATCH: test.sv:6:5: Latch inferred for signal 'c' (not all control paths of combinational always assign a value)
                           : ... Suggest use of always_latch for intentional latches
-node: ALWAYS 0x5555555d3ad0 <e1997#> {e6af} [always_comb]
    6 |     always_comb
      |     ^~~~~~~~~~~
                ... For warning description see https://verilator.org/warn/LATCH?v=5.045
                ... Use "/* verilator lint_off LATCH */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
case (b)
    2'd0, 2'd1, 2'd2: c = '0;
    default:;
endcase
- V3Active.cpp:626:   activeAll:
-arning-suppressed-LATCH: test.sv:6:5: Latch inferred for signal 'c' (not all control paths of combinational always assign a value)...
case (b)
    0: c = '0;
endcase
